title: Why I spent February coding an alternative to trading spreadsheets
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Hi everyone, as a developer, I finally accepted that my brain (and Excel) wasn't cut out for high-performance trading. I was fed up with 15 MB files that froze my browser when I tried to enter a trade.

So I built Hercul Market [https://hercul-market.vercel.app](https://hercul-market.vercel.app) . The goal is simple: to transform the chaos of a trading journal into a data-driven dashboard.

What the tool already does (and why it's better than an .xlsx):

AI structure analysis: It identifies Order Blocks and liquidity zones in seconds.

Real performance calculation: Automation of IRR and drawdown without broken formulas.

Zero Manual Input: Everything is synchronized via PostgreSQL, ready for backtesting.

It's currently in free beta. I'm looking for user feedback (criticism welcome!) to know whether I should prioritize adding new pairs or polishing the risk management dashboard.

If you want to stop managing your savings by guesswork, the link is just above.

What do you use to track your stats? A homemade script, SaaS, or good old Excel? 📈

comments.author: Opening_Kitchen_5349
comments.body: I’ve been using SuperTrader for trade tracking, which has been really helpful. I’ve also tried Tradersync, but I’ve noticed a few downsides. The user interface can be a bit overwhelming, especially for beginners, and sometimes the syncing with my broker can be glitchy.
